channel.title: WildlifeGarden on Nature Works
channel.description: Recent content in WildlifeGarden on Nature Works

item.title: Ivy wall
item.description:
Ivy is a fantastic wildlife plant but do watch out where it’s growing

Ivy (Hedera helix) is a fantastic wildlife plant (supporting over 106 species of insect, as evergreen habitat, for its flowers and its berries. http://localhost:1313/blog/240129-ivy-wall/

item.title: What Wild Things Where?
item.description:
I had a brilliant email reply from Linda at Buzz Club answering some of my habitat questions

John Little is one of my favourite gardeners and hole diggers, as he is constantly experiments with wildlife habitat and aesthetic designs (see his Tiktok channel). http://localhost:1313/blog/231225-what-wild-things/

item.title: Stone Birdbath
item.description:
A heavy birdbath that can be easily cleaned is great for birds & can be made from natural materials

I was reading about birdbaths in the New York Times (warning: paywall). http://localhost:1313/blog/231014-stone-birdbath/

item.title: Radical Horticulture
item.description:
Gardens are irrevocably connected to society, and we need a radical shake-up of both

I read Benjamin Vogt’s A New Garden Ethic a few years ago (it’s brililant, have a read), and he continues joining up the dots between society, politics, economics and gardening with native plants. http://localhost:1313/blog/230805-radical-horticulture/

item.title: Habitat
item.description:
List of diverse wildlife habitat ideas

This is simply a list of wildlife habitat structures, for my own benefit. http://localhost:1313/habitat/

item.title: Apple pots
item.description:
I took the apple trees to the primary school and talked to the pupils about grafting, clones and propagation

Work continues on the wildlife food forest, albeit slowly, at my local primary school. http://localhost:1313/blog/230323-apple-pots/

item.title: My Wildlife Allotment
item.description:
Nadine Mitschunas has created the most amazing wildlife allotments, and writes a blog for the Hardy Plant Society

I first saw My Wildlife Allotment on BBC Gardeners World a couple of years ago, and was impressed then with the visual richness of the allotment. http://localhost:1313/blog/230227-wildlife-allotment/

item.title: My take on Planting in a Post-wild World
item.description: My entry into gardening was via the forest garden, so I find the emphasis on the ornamental in mainstream gardening quite perplexing, particularly after reading Benjamin Vogt’s A New Garden Ethic. http://localhost:1313/blog/planting-post-wild-world/

item.title: Wildlife features
item.description:
Checklist of invaluable features for your wildlife friendly garden

A list of features that benefit wildlife in any garden. http://localhost:1313/wildlife-features/

item.title: Wildlife garden checklist
item.description:
Inspired by Malcolm Berry’s talk, here’s a simple checklist of invaluable features for your wildlife-friendly garden

Inspired by Malcolm Berry’s wildlife gardening talk to Drefach Gardening Club, here is a simple list of features that benefit wildlife in any garden. http://localhost:1313/blog/wildlife-garden-checklist/

item.title: DBIF
item.description:
An invaluable tool in the wildlife gardeners’ toolbox, The Database of Insects and their Food Plants details scientifically corroborated interactions between insects and plants

The Database of Insects and their Food Plants (DBIF for short) is a fantastic resource for wildlife gardeners. http://localhost:1313/dbif/
